pattern: Since value can only flow leftward, the best achievable maximum is the largest ceiling of a running prefix average across the array.
hints: An operation moves one unit from a position to its left neighbor, so a prefix of the array can only redistribute its total among its own elements.
For the first i+1 elements holding sum S, the smallest possible maximum within them is the ceiling of S divided by (i+1).
Sweep left to right keeping the running sum; the answer is the maximum over all prefixes of ceil(prefix_sum / prefix_length).

edge_inputs.description: value can be smoothed to a lower max
leftmost is the largest and cannot be reduced
big tail cannot help an already-large head
ascending values average upward with ceiling

stress.description: long random array across the full non-negative range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: exercises the prefix-average ceiling over varied distributions
